Jerubbaal, a symbolic name of Gideon
Midjan, a son of Abraham; also his country and (collectively) his descendants
Moreh, a Canaanite; also a hill (perhaps named from him)
an encampment (of travellers or troops); hence, an army, whether literal (of soldiers) or figurative (of dancers, angels, cattle, locusts, stars; or even the sacred courts)
En-Charod, a place in Palestine
a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
a vale (i.e. broad depression)
properly, hidden, i.e. dark; used only of the north as aquarter (gloomy and unknown)

tokens.text: that were with him rose up early
tokens.range: literally, to load up (on the back of man or beast), i.e. to start early in the morning
Gidon, an Israelite
to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong (figuratively, courageous, causatively strengthen, cure, help, repair, fortify), obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer
drought; also a cutting instrument (from its destructive effect), as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement
a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.),

Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
the right hand or side (leg, eye) of a person or other object (as the stronger and more dexterous); locally, the south
properly, a pail; but generally of earthenware; a jar  for domestic purposes
a flambeau, lamp or flame
to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)
the head (as most easily shaken), whether literal or figurative (in many applications, of place, time, rank, itc.)
to burst (literally or figuratively)
a cornet (as giving a clear sound) or curved horn
a cornet (as giving a clear sound) or curved horn
three; occasionally (ordinal) third, or (multiple) thrice
properly, dark (as enveloped), i.e. the north; hence (by orientation), the left hand
to clatter, i.e. slap (the hands together), clang (an instrument); by analogy, to drive (a nail or tent-pin, a dart, etc.); by implication, to become bondsman by handclasping)
to clatter, i.e. slap (the hands together), clang (an instrument); by analogy, to drive (a nail or tent-pin, a dart, etc.); by implication, to become bondsman by handclasping)

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

20.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: fearing; morally, reverent
